title: quercus parvula var. tamalpaisensis
intro: Quercus parvula var. tamalpaisensis, also known as the Mount Tamalpais oak, is a rare subspecies of the Coast live oak (Quercus agrifolia) endemic to Mount Tamalpais in Marin County, California. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus parvula var. tamalpaisensis include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote healthy aging.
